lines.english: The True Lord is the social status and honor of the Gurmukh.
As Gurmukh, O my mind, remember the Naam, the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਨਿਬਹੈ ਚਲੈ ਤੇਰੈ ਨਾਲਿ ॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sadaa nibahai chalai terai naal | rahaau |
lines.english: It shall stand by you always, and go with you. ||Pause||
Within the Gurmukh, is God, his friend and helper. ||2||
He Himself blesses the Gurmukh with greatness. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਆਪਿ ਕਰੇ ਸੋ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh jis no aap kare so hoe |
lines.english: He alone becomes Gurmukh, whom the Lord so blesses.
The Gurmukh, O Nanak, emancipates his family and relations. ||4||6||
The Gurmukh lives the True Word of the Shabad, and practices good deeds.
Wadahans, Third Mehl:
My mind is satisfied, meditating on the Name of the Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਸਨਾ ਹਰਿ ਸਾਦਿ ਲਗੀ ਸਹਜਿ ਸੁਭਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: rasanaa har saad lagee sehaj subhaae |
lines.english: My tongue is intuitively attracted to the taste of the Lord.
My eyes are content, lovingly focused on the One Lord.
Lasting peace is obtained, contemplating the Shabad, the True Word of God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਣੇ ਸਤਗੁਰ ਵਿਟਹੁ ਸਦਾ ਬਲਿਹਾਰੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aapane satagur vittahu sadaa balihaaree |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I am forever a sacrifice to my True Guru. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਹ ਸਰੀਰਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: deh sareer sukh hovai sabad har naae |
lines.english: The frame of my body is at peace, through the Shabad, and the Name of the Lord.
My mind is content, having forsaken the love of duality. ||2||
The fragrance of the Naam permeates my heart. ||3||
O Nanak, one who has such great destiny written upon his forehead,
through the Bani of the Guru's Word, easily and intuitively becomes free of desire. ||4||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰੇ ਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਨਾਮੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: poore gur te naam paaeaa jaae |
lines.english: From the Perfect Guru, the Naam is obtained.
Wadahans, Third Mehl:
Through the Shabad, the True Word of God, one merges in the True Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏ ਮਨ ਨਾਮੁ ਨਿਧਾਨੁ ਤੂ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: e man naam nidhaan too paae |
lines.english: O my soul, obtain the treasure of the Naam,

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਮੈਲੁ ਗਵਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai sabad vichahu mail gavaae |
lines.english: Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, filth is washed away from within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਣੇ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਮੰਨਿ ਲੈ ਰਜਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aapane gur kee man lai rajaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: by submitting to the Will of your Guru. ||1||Pause||
It dies, and is born again, and is ruined by the Messenger of Death. ||3||
Deluded by doubt, the world wanders around.
The Immaculate Naam comes to abide within the mind. ||2||
By Guru's Grace, they enshrine the Name within their minds. ||4||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸੇ ਵਡਭਾਗੀ ਜਿਨ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: naanak se vaddabhaagee jin har naam dhiaaeaa |
lines.english: O Nanak, very fortunate are those who meditate on the Name of the Lord.
Wadahans, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਸੇਵਾ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਤਾ ਮਨੁ ਬਿਰਥਾ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: haumai vich sevaa na hovee taa man birathaa jaae |1|
lines.english: In egotism, selfless service cannot be performed, and so the soul goes unfulfilled. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਨਾਵੈ ਨਾਲਿ ਵਿਰੋਧੁ ਹੈ ਦੁਇ ਨ ਵਸਹਿ ਇਕ ਠਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: haumai naavai naal virodh hai due na vaseh ik tthaae |
lines.english: Ego is opposed to the Name of the Lord; the two do not dwell in the same place.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਚੇਤਿ ਮਨ ਮੇਰੇ ਤੂ ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਕਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: har chet man mere too gur kaa sabad kamaae |
lines.english: O my mind, think of the Lord, and practice the Word of the Guru's Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੁ ਮੰਨਹਿ ਤਾ ਹਰਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਤਾ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਹਉਮੈ ਜਾਇ ॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: hukam maneh taa har milai taa vichahu haumai jaae | rahaau |
lines.english: If you submit to the Hukam of the Lord's Command, then you shall meet with the Lord; only then will your ego depart from within. ||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਸਭੁ ਸਰੀਰੁ ਹੈ ਹਉਮੈ ਓਪਤਿ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: haumai sabh sareer hai haumai opat hoe |
lines.english: Egotism is within all bodies; through egotism, we come to be born.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਵਡਾ ਗੁਬਾਰੁ ਹੈ ਹਉਮੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਬੁਝਿ ਨ ਸਕੈ ਕੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: haumai vaddaa gubaar hai haumai vich bujh na sakai koe |2|
lines.english: Egotism is total darkness; in egotism, no one can understand anything. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਜੀਉ ਬੰਧੁ ਹੈ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਵਸੈ ਮਨਿ ਆਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: haumai vich jeeo bandh hai naam na vasai man aae |3|
lines.english: In egotism, the soul is in bondage, and the Naam, the Name of the Lord, does not come to abide in the mind. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਨ ਬੁਝਿਆ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: haumai vich bhagat na hovee hukam na bujhiaa jaae |
lines.english: In egotism, devotional worship cannot be performed, and the Hukam of the Lord's Command cannot be understood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤਗੁਰਿ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਹਉਮੈ ਗਈ ਤਾ ਸਚੁ ਵਸਿਆ ਮਨਿ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: naanak satagur miliai haumai gee taa sach vasiaa man aae |
lines.english: O Nanak, meeting with the True Guru, egotism is eliminated, and then, the True Lord comes to dwell in the mind||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਕਮਾਵੈ ਸਚਿ ਰਹੈ ਸਚੇ ਸੇਵਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੪॥੯॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: sach kamaavai sach rahai sache sev samaae |4|9|12|
lines.english: One starts practicing truth, abides in truth and by serving the True One gets absorbed in Him. ||4||9||12||
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Wadahans, Fourth Mehl, First House:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਮਿਲਣ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਮਨਿ ਆਸਾ ॥
lines.translit: mai prabh milan prem man aasaa |
lines.english: My mind longs to meet my Beloved Lord.
The Gurmukh enjoys the Lord, the ocean of peace. ||1||
There is one bed, and One Lord God.
